article_title: Investors Press Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to Disclose Data Center Water and Power Use
summary: Submission content is reviewable, but the PR branch includes unrelated submissions and cannot be merged as-is

findings.message: PR branch contains unrelated submission files
findings.details: PR #953 is titled and branched for the data-center water and power disclosure submission, but it also includes src/content/submissions/2026-04/2026-04-19T19-16-45Z_rust-195-adds-cfg_select-extends-if-let-guards-and.json and src/content/submissions/2026-04/2026-04-19T19-16-40Z_india-sets-up-aigeg-to-centralize-ai-governance-an.json. Those belong to other review flows, and the India PR (#958) has already been merged. Merging #953 as-is would create a contaminated merge.

content_preview.title: Investors Press Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to Disclose Data Center Water and Power Use
content_preview.summary: Shareholders are asking Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to publish site-specific data-center water and energy data as scrutiny of AI infrastructure grows.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

More than a dozen investors are pressing Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet's Google to publish site-specific data-center water and energy data before spring annual meetings, according to [Tom's Hardware](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/investors-push-amazon-microsoft-and-google-to-disclose-data-center-water-and-power-consumption), which summarized Reuters reporting. [TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/pro/we-havent-seen-them-disclosing-enough-about-their-water-consumption...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
Clean the PR branch so it contains only the intended data-center disclosure submission before resubmitting for merge

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is concise and clearly scoped. It avoids overstating what the investor resolutions would immediately change and keeps the corporate sustainability claims separated from the shareholder-pressure reporting.
editor_notes.source_verification: I read all five listed sources directly. Tom's Hardware and TechRadar support the shareholder push for site-specific disclosure ahead of annual meetings. Amazon's sustainability page supports the AWS water-positive-by-2030 commitment. Google's data center water page supports the claim that it reports annual water use for each data center location and each replenishment project. Microsoft's sustainability report supports the direct-to-chip cooling water-savings claim and the company's water-positive-by-2030 goal.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: For the intended article, the claims are supported by the listed sources and the analysis stays within the available evidence.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The piece frames the comparison gap as an inference grounded in the different disclosure formats described by the sources.
editor_notes.originality: Originality check passed for this exact investor-pressure and site-level disclosure angle.
concerns: The PR is structurally invalid because it contains unrelated submission files in addition to the intended article.
One of the unrelated files corresponds to India AIGEG, whose own PR (#958) has already merged, so merging this branch would produce a polluted history.
recommendations: Remove the unrelated Rust and India submission files from PR #953 and keep only the data-center disclosure submission.
Resubmit or update the PR branch, then request re-review.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Request changes. The article itself is reviewable, but the PR cannot be merged until the branch is cleaned up.
